text.title: Pain-Point Research Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Pain-Point Research application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Each pain entry carries a public URL to the source post / review. Pains without URL are rejected.
rule.rationale: Pains without links cannot be cross-checked or re-cited.

rule.statement: Each pain carries a frequency proxy (thread upvotes / repeat appearances across sources / reviewer count). Single-mention pains are flagged 'anecdotal'.
rule.rationale: Single-mention pains over-index on outlier opinion.

rule.statement: Each pain names the current workaround (custom script, paid tool, spreadsheet, manual process, none). Workaround-quality drives monetisability.
rule.rationale: Pains with strong workarounds are weak signals; absent workarounds are stronger.

rule.statement: Pains scored on five criteria (frequency / cost / urgency / workaround quality / monetisability). 1–5 scale with one-sentence rationale per row.
rule.rationale: Single-metric scoring (frequency only) misses urgency and willingness-to-pay.

rule.statement: Each pain carries a segment tag from the operator's segment map. 'All users' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Cross-segment pains hide where the pain is actually monetisable.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
